channel.title: +sankIrNa misc on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +sankIrNa misc on Vishvas's notes

item.title: Buḍḍu Saheb Climbs a Hill
item.description: [[Buḍḍu Saheb Climbs a Hill Source: prekshaa]] Janab Muhammad Buḍan Khān was the inspector of Urdu schools. He would come to inspect the Anglo-Hindustani school in our town 1 once or twice a year. Muslims citizens were a sizable minority 2 in our town, so they had a separate school. In that school, there were two Hindu teachers who taught Kannada. The strength of the school was about a hundred. The headmaster of the school was a person by name Janab Mustafā.

item.title: K V Ramaswami Iyer (
item.description: K V Ramaswami Iyer (Part 1) [[K V Ramaswami Iyer (Part 1) Source: prekshaa]] I have already written about my Sanskrit teacher, Kashi Raghavendracharya elsewhere. 1 I shall now move on to reminiscences of my English teacher. It appears that Mulbagal secured the benefit of English education during 1900. Before this, there were only two or three people literate in English apart from those employed by the Post Office. One of them was Ramachandracharya from Kaveripattinam.

item.title: Kolar Lodging
item.description: [[Kolar Lodging Source: prekshaa]] Upon completing my first year of high school (Fourth Form) at Mysore, I moved to Kolar for the second year (Fifth Form). After initial hardships in matters of boarding and lodging, I was able to set up boarding arrangement at one Venkataramayya’s residence, which was made possible by Ramadasappa’s munificence. Ramadasappa and Venkataramayya were friends. While they were not related to one another, they belonged to the same community (Baḍaganāḍu) and adhered to a similar lifestyle and culture.

item.title: The Obscure Shiva Bhakta: Shiva Puje Naranappa
item.description: [[The Obscure Shiva Bhakta: Shiva Puje Naranappa Source: prekshaa]] Earlier in this series, I had written about my maternal grandmother Sakamma. Naranappa was her husband. I’m unaware of how he was transferred to the Mulubagilu region or where he was employed prior to arriving here. He was appointed as the Sheikhdar of the Baikur Hobli, Mulubagilu Taluk. It appears that in those days, the elders of my family didn’t know he hailed from my own community.
